title: Code Review: Add task creation modal

segments.narration: This PR adds a modal dialog for creating tasks. Notice the modal div has no ARIA attributes—screen readers won't announce this as a dialog.
The close button is a span with onclick. Keyboard users can't reach it because spans aren't focusable—this should be a button element.
These form inputs lack label elements. Screen reader users only hear 'edit blank' with no context about what each field is for.
The priority dropdown and submit button also lack proper labels. Placeholder text disappears when you type, leaving no visual label.
When the modal opens, there's no focus management. Keyboard users can tab right past the modal to background content—a critical navigation trap.
The close function has no Escape key handler. Keyboard users are stuck—they can't close the modal without a mouse.
Error messages appear visually but lack role equals alert. Screen readers won't announce validation errors, leaving users confused about what went wrong.
The close button styling makes it look clickable, but remember—it's a span, not a button. This creates a false affordance for keyboard users.
The border color triple A gray and placeholder color triple A gray fail WCAG contrast requirements. Low vision users struggle to see these elements.
The council found fourteen WCAG violations, seven of them critical. This modal is completely unusable for keyboard and screen reader users.
To fix this, add role equals dialog, aria modal equals true, and aria labelledby pointing to the heading. This tells assistive tech it's a dialog.
The verdict is clear: BLOCK. Accessibility isn't optional polish—it's a legal requirement that affects millions of users. This needs fixes before shipping.
